# 'repo sync' should not restack upstacks without --restack flag.
# This is the fix for issue #1134.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-12-28T18:14:15Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# Create a stack: main -> feat1 -> feat2
git add feat1.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature 1' feat1

git add feat2.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature 2' feat2

# Submit the entire stack
gs stack submit --fill
stderr 'Created #1'
stderr 'Created #2'

# Merge feat1 (the downstack)
shamhub merge alice/example 1

# Sync without --restack
# This should delete feat1 and update feat2's base to main
# but NOT rebase feat2 onto main.
gs repo sync
stderr '#1 was merged'

# feat2 should still exist.
# The git graph should show feat2 NOT rebased onto main
# (still on top of feat1's original commit).
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph-after-sync.txt

# Now explicitly restack to actually rebase
gs repo sync --restack

git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph-after-restack.txt

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feature 1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feature 2

-- golden/graph-after-sync.txt --
* 1715fe8 (HEAD -> feat2, origin/feat2) Add feature 2
| *   052886d (origin/main, main) Merge change #1
| |\  
| |/  
|/|   
* | 4be7a61 Add feature 1
|/  
* e41b284 Initial commit
-- golden/graph-after-restack.txt --
* 105ff59 (HEAD -> feat2) Add feature 2
*   052886d (origin/main, main) Merge change #1
|\  
| * 4be7a61 Add feature 1
|/  
* e41b284 Initial commit
